subject: Thread thr-0000 re Ravi
body_text:
Amara,

Hope you had a good weekend. I wanted to reach out because I've been tracking a company I think might be right in your wheelhouse – [Terraform Dynamics](companies/terraform-dynamics). They're doing some interesting work combining satellite imagery with foundation models for carbon credit verification, and the founding team has serious technical depth.

I know climate AI infrastructure is core to your thesis at Halfway, and this feels like it could be a fit for your seed mandate. We're not actively pursuing it given our stage focus, but thought you might want to take a look before their round fills up.

Happy to make an intro if you're interested. Let me know.

Best,
Ravi

subject: Thread thr-0000 re Bill
body_text:
Bill,

Thank you so much for the introduction to the [Terraform Industries](companies/terraform-industries) team last week — the conversation was incredibly productive, and I think there's real potential for us to participate in their upcoming round. Their approach to synthetic fuel infrastructure aligns perfectly with our climate thesis at Halfway.

I'd love to grab coffee next time you're in the city and pick your brain on a few other deals we're evaluating in the carbon capture space. Your perspective on hardware-heavy climate ventures has always been invaluable to me, especially given how the market has shifted since last year.

Let me know what your schedule looks like over the next couple of weeks.

Gratefully,
Amara

subject: Thread thr-0001 re Marcus
body_text:
Hey Amara,

Great running into you at the Menlo event last week — always good to catch up with someone who actually understands the infrastructure layer. I wanted to follow up on our conversation about the [Terraform Industries](companies/terraform-industries) deal. We ended up passing at our partnership meeting, but I know you were circling them for your climate thesis at Halfway.

If you're still looking at the round, happy to share our diligence notes. Their synthetic fuels approach is compelling, though we had some questions around their scaling timeline that might be worth pressure-testing.

Let me know if you want to grab coffee next week and dig in.

Best,
Marcus

subject: Thread thr-0001 re Jordan
body_text:
Thanks for sending over the updated deck, Jordan. I took a closer look at the technical architecture section and I'm impressed by how you've thought through the inference optimization layer—it addresses several concerns our team raised during the last call.

I've shared the materials with our technical advisor and she should have feedback by end of week. In the meantime, I'd love to schedule a follow-up with you and your CTO to dig deeper into the infrastructure cost projections, particularly around the GPU cluster scaling assumptions.

Also, I noticed you're planning to present at the [Climate Tech Summit](events/climate-tech-summit-2026) next month—let me know if you'd like introductions to any of the LPs attending. We've had good luck making connections there in past years.

Looking forward to continuing the conversation.

Amara

subject: Thread thr-0002 re Bill
body_text:
Amara,

Hope you're doing well. I was thinking about our conversation last month regarding [Helios Climate Systems](companies/helios-climate-systems) and wanted to share some context from my end. I actually led their pre-seed back in Q3 2023 – we came in at 8% equity for that initial $1.2M check, which gave us a board observer seat.

I know there's been some confusion about the early cap table since Marcus came on, but I wanted to make sure you had the full picture before your due diligence goes too deep. The founding team is solid, and I think this fits squarely in your climate infrastructure thesis.

Let me know if you want to grab coffee this week to discuss – I'm in the city through Thursday.

Best,
Bill

subject: Thread thr-0002 re Sarah
body_text:
Sarah,

Thanks for the detailed breakdown on the [Helion Energy](orgs/helion-energy) deal structure — your notes on the milestone-based tranches were exactly what I needed to pressure-test our internal model. I've shared the revised memo with the partnership and we're discussing it at Thursday's IC meeting.

Quick ask: would you have 30 minutes this week to walk me through your thinking on the IP licensing terms? I want to make sure we're not missing anything before we finalize our position.

Also, separately, I'd love to get your read on a new climate infrastructure pitch we received — early-stage fusion supply chain play. Happy to send over the deck if you're interested.

Talk soon,
Amara

subject: Thread thr-0003 re Tomoko
body_text:
Hi Amara,

Great catching up at the Climate Tech Summit last week – your panel on infrastructure bottlenecks was one of the highlights. I wanted to follow up on our conversation about the carbon accounting space. We've been tracking [Watershed](companies/watershed) closely and I know you've had some exposure to similar plays in your portfolio. Would love to compare notes on what you're seeing in terms of enterprise adoption patterns and whether the regulatory tailwinds in Europe are translating to real budget allocation yet. 

If you have 30 minutes in the next couple weeks, I'd be happy to share some of our internal research on the space. Let me know what works on your end.

Best,
Tomoko

subject: Thread thr-0003 re Marcus
body_text:
Hey Amara,

Thanks for the quick reply on the [Halfway Capital](org/halfway-capital) co-invest question. I double-checked with our climate team and we're still very much interested in participating in the Terraform Dynamics round—Ben is still leading our climate infrastructure thesis, so he'll be the point person on our side. Given where their carbon capture tech is right now, I think the $4M allocation we discussed makes sense, though we might push for slightly more favorable terms on the pro-rata.

Can you send over the data room access when you get a chance? Happy to sync later this week once we've had a chance to dig into the financials.

Best,
Marcus

subject: Thread thr-0004 re Lena
body_text:
Hey Lena,

Great catching up at the Climate Tech Summit last week – your comments on the infrastructure bottleneck panel really resonated with me. I've been thinking about what you said regarding compute constraints for climate modeling startups, and it dovetails with some seed-stage deals we're seeing at [Halfway Capital](halfway-capital).

There's a company I'm doing diligence on that's building optimized inference infrastructure specifically for earth system models – feels like it sits right at the intersection of our respective theses. Would love to get your read on the space if you have 20 minutes in the next couple weeks.

Also happy to share our internal memo on the AI-for-climate landscape if that's useful. Let me know what works for your schedule.

Best,
Amara

subject: Thread thr-0004 re Mina
body_text:
Amara,

Thanks for the quick reply on this. I've been doing more digging into [Fervo Energy](companies/fervo-energy) and their enhanced geothermal approach, and I think there's a real opportunity here for a co-investment if you're still interested.

Their Series B is moving fast—I'm hearing they'll close allocation by end of month. Given your climate focus at Halfway and our prior work together, I wanted to loop you in before it gets competitive.

I can send over my internal memo and the deck if helpful. Let me know if you want to jump on a call this week to discuss.

Best,
Mina

subject: Thread thr-0005 re Bill
body_text:
Amara,

Hope this finds you well. I was catching up on some of the latest climate infrastructure deals and noticed [Halfway Capital](halfway-capital) led the seed round for that geothermal startup out of Austin — nice work getting in early on that one.

I wanted to reach out because I'm seeing some interesting overlap between the AI infra plays you've been tracking and a few things crossing my desk. There's a company doing inference optimization for edge devices that might fit your thesis perfectly. Would love to get your take on it over coffee next week if you have time.

Also, my daughter just got into Stanford's sustainability program, so I might be picking your brain on the climate tech landscape from a different angle soon.

Let me know what your schedule looks like.

Best,
Bill

subject: Thread thr-0005 re Kofi
body_text:
Amara, thanks for the quick reply on this.

Just to clarify from our earlier discussion – I believe the [Helios Climate Systems](companies/helios-climate-systems) seed round actually closed at $4.2M, not the $3.8M figure you mentioned. I was on the call with their CFO last week and he confirmed the final number after they added that late strategic investor.

Anyway, happy to sync on the Series A co-lead structure whenever works for you. I've got some bandwidth Thursday afternoon if you want to jump on a quick call.

– Kofi

subject: Thread thr-0006 re Jordan
body_text:
Hi Amara,

Following up on our conversation last week about the infrastructure layer we're building at [NovaMind AI](companies/novamind-ai). I wanted to share that we just closed our first enterprise pilot with a Fortune 500 logistics company—they're seeing 40% latency improvements on their inference workloads using our adaptive compute allocation system.

We're targeting a $4M seed round to expand the engineering team and build out our multi-cloud orchestration capabilities. Given [Halfway Capital](companies/halfway-capital)'s focus on AI infrastructure, I think there could be strong alignment here.

Would you have 30 minutes next week to dive deeper into our technical architecture and go-to-market strategy? Happy to work around your schedule.

Best,
Jordan

subject: Thread thr-0006 re Marcus
body_text:
Marcus,

Thanks for the heads up on the [Terraform Energy](companies/terraform-energy) round—I had a feeling they'd move quickly given the traction they're seeing in the industrial heat pump space. We're still doing diligence on our end but leaning toward participating at the allocation you mentioned. Let me sync with my partners this week and I'll have a definitive answer by Friday.

Separately, would love to grab coffee next time you're in SF. Been meaning to pick your brain on how you're thinking about the AI infrastructure buildout and where climate intersects—feels like there's more overlap than either ecosystem fully appreciates yet.

Best,
Amara

subject: Thread thr-0007 re Elena
body_text:
Amara,

Hope you're having a good week! I wanted to reach out because we're seeing some interesting deal flow in the climate infrastructure space and I immediately thought of you. There's a company called [GridScale](companies/gridscale) that's building AI-driven load balancing for renewable microgrids—right in your wheelhouse from what I understand.

They're raising a seed round and the founding team came out of Google DeepMind's energy optimization group. I know Halfway typically leads at this stage, and we'd be interested in potentially co-investing if it's a fit. Would you have 20 minutes this week or next to compare notes?

Let me know what works on your end.

Best,
Elena

body_text:

Thanks for the quick turnaround on those comps—really helpful to see how the Series A multiples have shifted since Q4. I ran the numbers against our internal model for [Terraform Energy](companies/terraform-energy) and I think we're closer to alignment than I initially expected.

I'm planning to be in SF the week of the 28th for a board meeting. Would be great to grab coffee and dig into the co-invest structure you mentioned. I know we've danced around terms before, but this one feels like it could be a good fit for both our mandates.

Best,

subject: Thread thr-0008 re Jordan
body_text:
Thanks for the quick follow-up, Amara. I've attached the updated financial projections you requested—we've refined our assumptions around GPU cluster costs based on the latest pricing from our [CoreWeave](companies/coreweave) contract.

To answer your question about our hiring timeline: we're planning to bring on two senior ML engineers by end of Q2, assuming we close this round by early May. The candidates we're in final stages with both have experience scaling inference infrastructure, which should directly address the concerns you raised about operational readiness.

Happy to jump on a call this week if you want to walk through the numbers together. Let me know what works on your end.

Best,
Jordan

subject: Thread thr-0009 re Mina
body_text:
Hey Amara,

Hope you had a great weekend! I wanted to loop back on our conversation from last week about [Threshold Ventures](people/mina-kapoor)'s new climate fund. We're seeing a lot of overlap in our deal flow lately, particularly around carbon accounting infrastructure and grid optimization plays.

Hope this finds you well! I wanted to follow up on our conversation from last week about the infrastructure challenges we're tackling at [NovaMind](companies/novamind). subject: Thread thr-0012 re Priya
body_text:
Good morning, Priya,

Thanks for the quick reply on the [GridScale AI](companies/gridscale-ai) round—I appreciate you sharing your perspective on their unit economics. Your point about the margin compression risk in the current power purchase agreement structure is well taken, and I've asked their CFO to walk us through their hedging strategy in more detail.

I'm still leaning toward leading the Series A if we can get comfortable on that front, but I'd welcome the chance to co-invest if Sequoia is interested in taking a smaller allocation. Let me know if you'd like to jump on a call early next week to compare notes before term sheets go out.

Best,
Amara

subject: Thread thr-0013 re Lena
body_text:
Hey Lena,

Great catching up at the Climate Tech Summit last week—your panel on carbon accounting infrastructure was really thought-provoking. I wanted to follow up on our conversation about [Terraform Industries](companies/terraform-industries) and their approach to synthetic fuel production. We've been tracking them closely at Halfway, and I'm curious whether Initialized has looked at any of the adjacent plays in the electrolyzer space.

subject: Thread thr-0013 re Nadia
body_text:
Amara,

Thanks for the thoughtful note and for sharing your diligence memo on [Axiom Infrastructure](axiom-infrastructure) — I read through it over the weekend. Your instincts on the founding team's technical depth are spot on, and I think the TAM analysis holds up well even under more conservative assumptions.

One thing I'd push you to dig deeper on: their go-to-market timeline feels aggressive given current permitting bottlenecks in the markets they're targeting. Have you had a chance to talk with anyone at DOE who might have visibility into how those timelines are actually playing out?

Happy to jump on a call next week if you want to think through the competitive dynamics together. These infrastructure plays are tricky, but when they work, they really work.

Nadia

subject: Thread thr-0014 re Anna
body_text:

Hope you're doing well! Happy to make an intro if you're interested—I have a loose connection through my LP network. Let me know, and let's find time to catch up properly soon. It's been too long since our last coffee.

Warmly,

body_text:

Thanks for the thoughtful update on [Halfway Capital](organizations/halfway-capital)'s current pipeline—always impressed by how methodical you've been since we first connected back in your associate days. The climate infrastructure thesis is playing out exactly as we discussed last year.

Thanks for the quick turnaround on those notes from the climate summit last week—really appreciated your candid take on the panel discussions. I wanted to follow up on our conversation about [Terraform Energy](companies/terraform-energy) since we're both tracking their Series A. My partners are leaning toward passing given the cap table complexity, but I know you had a different read on their tech differentiation.

Would love to get 30 minutes on your calendar this week to compare notes before their deadline on the 28th. Also happy to share our diligence on their grid integration claims if that's useful.

Let me know what works.

Diego

subject: Thread thr-0023 re Jordan
body_text:
Hi Amara,

Thanks for the great conversation at the Climate Tech Summit last week—I really appreciated your perspective on the infrastructure gaps in carbon accounting. I wanted to follow up on our discussion about [Novamind](companies/novamind)'s approach to AI-powered emissions monitoring for industrial facilities.

We've just closed our pilot with two cement plants in the Midwest and the early results are promising: 23% improvement in detection accuracy compared to their existing sensor networks. I'd love to share our updated deck and discuss whether this might be a fit for Halfway's climate thesis at the seed stage.

Would you have 30 minutes sometime next week to dive deeper? Happy to work around your schedule.

Best,
Jordan Park
CEO & Co-founder, Novamind

subject: Thread thr-0023 re Lena
body_text:

Free for a call next week? I'm flexible Tuesday or Thursday afternoon.

Best,
Lena

subject: Thread thr-0024 re Tomoko
body_text:
Tomoko,

Great catching up at the Menlo event last week — your comments on the infrastructure bottlenecks for foundation models really stuck with me. I've been spending a lot of time lately looking at the intersection of AI compute and energy constraints, and I think there's a fascinating opportunity emerging in that space.

I wanted to flag a company we're currently evaluating called [Axiom Energy](companies/axiom-energy) — they're building modular power solutions specifically for edge AI deployments. Given Khosla's deep bench in both climate and AI, I wondered if this might be on your radar or if you'd seen competing approaches.

Would love to compare notes if you have 20 minutes this week. Happy to share our diligence materials in exchange for your perspective.

Best,
Amara

subject: Thread thr-0024 re Priya
body_text:
Hey Amara,

Thanks for the quick reply on [Priya Patel](people/priya-patel)'s availability. I actually just got back from a week in Tahoe, so apologies for the slight delay on my end as well.

I'd love to continue our conversation about co-investment opportunities in the climate infrastructure space—there's clearly some overlap in our thesis around grid optimization and carbon accounting platforms. Would next Thursday afternoon work for a call? I'm flexible after 2pm PT.

Also, separately, I've been hearing good things about a company called Terraform Dynamics that's working on AI-powered soil carbon measurement. Not sure if it's crossed your desk yet, but might be worth a look given Halfway's focus.

Let me know what works on timing.

Best,
Priya
